personas.name: Emotionally Overloaded Support Seeker
personas.description: A user who turns to the app during stress, loneliness, or anxiety and wants a safe, non-judgmental space to talk through feelings.
goals: Feel listened to and supported at any time
pain_points: Does not always have a trusted person available
Can feel dismissed or unsupported in daily life
context: Uses the app during emotionally difficult moments
May need immediate support outside normal therapy hours
constraints: Needs support without judgment or shame

personas.name: Self-Guided Coping Tool User
personas.description: A user who values structured coping exercises such as CBT prompts, breathing, journaling, and guided routines to regulate emotions independently.
pain_points: Gets frustrated if useful exercises are hard to find or locked
context: Uses the app for daily check-ins and emotional regulation
Often wants guided activities rather than passive reading
constraints: Exercises should be easy to start
Instructions should be specific and actionable

personas.name: Context-Aware Conversation Critic
personas.description: A user who expects the AI to understand prior messages, avoid repetition, and produce relevant, personalized responses instead of robotic dialogue.
goals: Receive responses that reflect the user's actual situation
pain_points: Responses feel generic, repetitive, or scripted
The chat loses context or misses what the user said
context: Uses the app as a conversational support tool
Compares the app against modern AI chat expectations
constraints: Needs responses that directly address the user's input

personas.name: Price-Conscious Mental Health User
personas.description: A user who wants affordable support, expects transparency about paid features, and is sensitive to misleading free-tier claims or aggressive upsells.
goals: Understand what is free before investing time
Access meaningful support without surprise paywalls
pain_points: Feels misled by pricing or free-trial messaging
Perceives subscriptions as too expensive for the value provided
context: May be comparing multiple low-cost or free mental health apps
May already be under financial stress
constraints: Pricing must be easy to understand
Upgrade prompts must not block essential use too early

personas.name: Reliability-Focused User in Distress
personas.description: A user who may already be stressed and needs the app to load quickly, preserve conversation state, and behave consistently without glitches.
goals: Use the app without technical interruptions
Trust that conversations and tools will load and persist correctly
pain_points: Slow loading, broken screens, and lost conversation history
Errors increase frustration during vulnerable moments
context: Often opens the app during urgent emotional need
May be using an older or slower mobile device
constraints: Core flows must remain stable under normal use
The app must recover gracefully from errors
